props.topic: HUMANITARIANS AI · The Anthropic Metaprompt: Task Description to Production Prompt

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. The metaprompt takes a plain two-sentence task description and outputs a full production prompt template. It applies Anthropic's prompting best practices automatically, delegating the first draft rather than skipping the work. The template has six components: role, context, input format, instructions, output format, and edge cases. It cannot know your schema or production failures, so generate, test on real inputs, and iterate before shipping.

It cannot know your schema or production failures, so generate, test on real inputs, and iterate before shipping.

beats.narration_text: Here is why The Anthropic Metaprompt: Task Description to Production Prompt belongs in your working knowledge. The systems, policies, and tools shaped by it affect decisions at your level — whether or not you understand the mechanism. Knowing the underlying logic gives you the frame; missing it means reasoning from output with no access to the inputs.

beats.narration_text: I like that the metaprompt turns two plain sentences into a six-component template but cannot know my production edge cases. I want to run the full loop on something real. Can you (1) take a two-sentence task description I give you and generate the six-component template, role through edge cases, (2) point out specifically which components it had to guess because my data schema was unknown, and (3) hand me three real inputs to test the draft on so I can see where it fails before I promote it?

props.segment: The Anthropic Metaprompt: Task Description to Production Prompt
props.command: A metaprompt is a prompt that writes prompts. Feed it a task description and it returns a full production template. The Anthropic metaprompt turns the blank-page problem into a structured generation problem.

beats.narration_text: The metaprompt has one input and one output. Input: a plain-language task description — two sentences, no structure required. Output: a complete prompt template with all the components a production prompt needs. The metaprompt applies Anthropic's own prompting best practices automatically. You are not skipping the work of prompt engineering — you are delegating the first draft to a system that has read the manual.

The generated template has six components. Role: who Claude is in this task. Context: what background it needs. Input format: how data arrives. Instructions: step-by-step task logic. Output format: what the response looks like. Edge cases: explicit handling for exceptions. All six are generated from your two-sentence description. You review, edit, and promote to production.

Use the metaprompt to bootstrap. Never ship its output without review. The metaprompt cannot know your specific data schema, your edge cases from production, or the failures you've already seen. It produces a strong first draft, not a final prompt. The workflow is: generate, test on real inputs, iterate. The blank page is gone. The judgment is still yours.

Your turn. Get the Anthropic metaprompt from the cookbooks repository. Feed it this task: 'Summarize a research paper into three bullet points for a non-specialist reader.' Read the generated template carefully. Find the component you would change first. Then test the template on an actual paper. That review step is the skill the metaprompt can't do for you. Kore, in for Humanitarians AI.
